title: Akamai Guardicore Segmentation Policy Change Audit Trail (`guardicore:policy`)
spl:
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:policy" earliest=-30d
| stats count values(change_type) as changes by admin_user, policy_name
| sort -count
description: Policy drift increases risk; auditing who relaxed rules supports least-privilege governance.
value: Compliance and security governance teams audit all microsegmentation policy changes, detect unauthorized relaxations that weaken east-west controls, and verify that changes occur within approved maintenance windows.
implementation: Install the add-on, configure API polling intervals appropriate to API limits, and map assets to CMDB for enrichment lookups.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.Change
  by Change.user, Change.object, Change.action span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunkbase — Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk

knownFalsePositives: Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a log of who changed the security rules inside our network, when they did it, and what they changed, so we can catch mistakes or unauthorized weakening of our defenses.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426) v3.x collecting policy and audit events from the Centra REST API.
• Data in `index=microseg` with `sourcetype=guardicore:policy`. Key fields: `change_type` (create/modify/delete), `policy_name`, `admin_user`, `source_ip`, `timestamp`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `affected_segments`.
• Centra audit logging must be enabled (default in v4.x+). The TA polls the audit API endpoint for policy change events.
• Create a `gc_admins.csv` lookup: `admin_user,role,team,approved_change_window` for mapping administrators to their authorized change windows.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify policy change events are arriving:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:policy" earliest=-30d
| stats count by change_type, admin_user
```
You should see create/modify/delete events attributed to named administrators. If empty: verify the TA's audit API input is enabled, and that the API account has audit-read permissions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Policy changes with risk assessment:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:policy" earliest=-30d
| eval risk=case(
    change_type="delete", "HIGH",
    match(lower(new_value), "(?i)any|allow.*all|0\.0\.0\.0"), "HIGH",
    change_type="modify" AND isnotnull(old_value), "MEDIUM",
    change_type="create", "LOW",
    1==1, "INFO"
)
| lookup gc_admins.csv admin_user OUTPUT role team approved_change_window
| eval outside_window=if(isnotnull(approved_change_window) AND NOT match(strftime(_time, "%H"), approved_change_window), "YES", "NO")
| stats count values(change_type) as changes values(policy_name) as policies by admin_user, risk, outside_window, team
| sort - risk, - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Classifies policy changes by risk level. Deletions are always high-risk (removing protection). Modifications that introduce overly broad rules (any/allow-all/0.0.0.0) are also high-risk — these bypass intended segmentation. Changes outside the administrator's approved window may indicate compromised credentials or unauthorized access.

Policy relaxation detection:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:policy" earliest=-7d
| where change_type="modify"
| eval relaxed=if(match(lower(new_value), "allow|permit|any") AND NOT match(lower(old_value), "allow|permit|any"), "YES", "NO")
| where relaxed="YES"
| table _time, admin_user, policy_name, old_value, new_value
| sort - _time
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Guardicore Centra: Administration > Audit Log. Compare the change entries with Splunk for the same time range.
(b) Make a test policy change (e.g., add a comment to a rule). Verify the event appears in Splunk with the correct `admin_user`, `change_type`, and `policy_name`.
(c) Verify admin mapping: spot-check 3 admin usernames against the `gc_admins.csv` lookup.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Guardicore — Policy Audit"):
• Row 1 — Single-value tiles: "Changes (30d)", "High-risk changes", "Outside-window changes".
• Row 2 — Timechart: changes by type over 30 days.
• Row 3 — Table: admin, change type, policy, risk, timestamp.

Alert: Trigger immediately for any HIGH-risk change (deletion or broad allow rule). Trigger for any change outside the approved window.

Runbook:
1. **Policy deleted** — Verify with the administrator. If unintentional: restore from Centra's policy version history. If intentional: document the business justification.
2. **Broad allow rule created** — This effectively disables segmentation for the affected segments. Confirm with the administrator and set a review deadline to narrow the rule.
3. **Change outside approved window** — Investigate whether the admin account was compromised. Check the source IP against known admin workstations.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No policy events** — The TA's audit input may not be enabled. Check the TA setup page and enable the Audit/Policy input.
• **`admin_user` shows a service account** — API-driven changes (e.g., from automation or orchestration) use service accounts. Ensure these are documented in `gc_admins.csv`.
• **`old_value` and `new_value` are empty** — Some change types (especially 'create') may not populate diff fields. Focus on 'modify' and 'delete' for diff analysis.
